description_long: This factory building is a mid-rise, rectangular brick structure with a flat roof and a repetitive grid of large, industrial-style windows along its main facade. The exterior is characterized by reddish-brown brickwork, with concrete or stone trim around the windows and at the roofline, giving it a utilitarian yet robust appearance. The building is flanked by a taller, modern beige structure and a smaller commercial unit, both of which are part of the same city block. The street-facing side features a main entrance and is bordered by a paved sidewalk and street, with streetlights and curbs visible. The roof appears clear of major machinery, and there are no visible smokestacks or loading dock doors, suggesting a light industrial or repurposed use. The overall style is reminiscent of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture, commonly found in urban factory districts.
description: A rectangular, mid-rise brick factory building with large industrial windows and a flat roof, set within a city block alongside taller and smaller commercial structures.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main brick facade with a grid of large industrial windows and the main entrance facing the street.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the depth of the building and the adjacent taller beige structure, with the smaller commercial unit in front.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the factory building, with fewer architectural details and the beige building more prominent.
description_view_3: The opposite side view emphasizes the black high-rise building and the side of the factory, with windows visible along the length.
